concerns.text: FR-002 requires administering SART and Ospan via a web interface. Tasks T014/T015 only implement local Python scoring functions and synthetic data generation. No task exists to implement the web interface, task delivery logic, or the interaction loop required to collect raw response data from participants.

FR-009 mandates a pre-study pilot check (n=5) with real participants to validate task functionality. Tasks T017/T018 only validate against synthetic data. There is no task to recruit, run, or analyze data from the required 5 human pilot participants.

FR-006 requires a fallback to Wilcoxon tests if bootstrapping fails. While T037 implements the Wilcoxon logic, no task exists to implement the 'convergence failure' detection logic that triggers the switch. Without this trigger, the system cannot fallback, risking a crash or silent failure.

SC-001 to SC-004 define success based on specific directional changes (e.g., reduction in SART errors). Task T043 validates against thresholds (p < 0.05, d ≥ 0.2) but does not explicitly mandate checking the *direction* of the effect, potentially allowing a significant result in the wrong direction to pass validation.

FR-006 and SC-005 require Holm-Bonferroni correction. Task T020 (Power Simulation) does not explicitly state that the simulation must account for the reduced alpha resulting from this correction, risking an inflated power estimate.
